focusedAudit.reason: 0.6.5 is a patch release of issue-driven correctness, responsiveness, packaging, and compatibility fixes. It introduces no new subsystem or graph-write path, so no focused change-cluster audit is required.

items.text: **AppImage builds now carry update information** (GH #222), so `appimageupdatetool` (and AppImageUpdate) can update Tine in place. Each release also publishes the matching `.zsync` file. Delta updates work from one release that has this metadata to the next, so the first usable update is the one after this release.

docs.reason: The changelog explains the new packaging capability; the Guide's application-use documentation remains current.

website.reason: This patch changes AppImage packaging rather than the website or product positioning.

blog.reason: Patch-release packaging improvement; no editorial post is required.

items.text: **Large graphs no longer do reference and rename work on the UI thread** (GH #233, #235, #236). Page-title blur now commits the same conflict-safe rename as Enter, repeated commits are suppressed, and reference lookups use a reconstructible in-memory candidate index with exact verification and a safe full-scan fallback. Linked References use Logseq-compatible disclosure defaults; Unlinked References precompute in the background while remaining collapsed.

docs.reason: The fixes restore documented page rename and reference behavior without changing the user-facing workflow.

website.reason: Correctness and responsiveness fixes do not change website claims or navigation.

blog.reason: Patch-release bug fixes; no editorial post is required.

items.text: **Opening Backups no longer hashes every saved snapshot** (GH #234). The list reads cheap manifest metadata in a blocking worker, while restoring still verifies the selected snapshot before changing graph files. The panel now exposes loading and retryable error states.

docs.reason: The existing Backups workflow remains unchanged; this fixes its responsiveness and preserves restore verification.

website.reason: The fix does not change website content or product positioning.

blog.reason: Patch-release bug fix; no editorial post is required.

items.text: **File-backed block rows now keep stable runtime identity** (GH #232) across cold loads, cache rebuilds, references, merges, and publish snapshots without adding synthetic `id::` properties.

docs.reason: This restores internal identity consistency without changing any documented user workflow.

website.reason: The internal correctness fix does not affect the website.

blog.reason: Patch-release correctness fix; no editorial post is required.

items.text: **IME composition now commits finalized block text once** (GH #237), avoiding intermediate graph writes and duplicate trailing-input commits.

docs.reason: The normal editing contract is unchanged; composition now follows it correctly.

website.reason: The editing fix does not change website content.

blog.reason: Patch-release editing fix; no editorial post is required.

items.text: **Template date expressions now understand natural language** (GH #226), so `<% next monday %>`, `in 5 days`, and similar English expressions expand to journal links using the graph's configured journal-title format. Unknown placeholders remain intact.

docs.reason: This parity fix extends accepted template expressions; the existing template documentation remains accurate.

website.reason: The template parity fix does not require a website change.

blog.reason: Patch-release parity fix; no editorial post is required.

items.text: **Reference-only page names now contribute namespace descendants** (GH #229), so the namespace sidebar, `{{namespace}}`, and a page's Hierarchy section show linked child paths even when no child file exists. All Pages remains file-only.

docs.reason: This restores namespace semantics without changing the documented namespace workflow.

website.reason: The namespace correctness fix does not require a website change.

blog.reason: Patch-release correctness fix; no editorial post is required.

items.text: **Android 9 can load Tine's native library** (GH #192). The earlier fix covered the backup path but missed a second `renameat2` call in the trash path, so v0.6.4 could still fail before launch. Both paths now use the API-compatible syscall entry point, and release CI checks the final APK for this class of loader regression.

docs.reason: This restores supported Android startup behavior; installation documentation remains current.

website.reason: The compatibility fix does not change website content.

blog.reason: Patch-release platform fix; no editorial post is required.

items.text: **Ctrl+F no longer reselects the query after every typed character** (GH #224). The field still selects its contents when Ctrl+F is invoked again, but ordinary debounced search updates leave the caret and selection alone.

docs.reason: This restores the expected in-page find interaction without changing its documented workflow.

website.reason: The interaction fix does not require a website change.

blog.reason: Patch-release interaction fix; no editorial post is required.

items.text: **Task slash commands replace the block's task marker** (GH #225), rather than appending literal text such as `DONE` after an existing `TODO`. The shared marker operation preserves priority and block properties.

docs.reason: This restores the expected slash-command behavior; task documentation remains current.

website.reason: The task-command fix does not require a website change.

blog.reason: Patch-release task fix; no editorial post is required.
